# Terraform MongoDB Atlas GCP Project Module

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-gcp-project.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-gcp-project/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-gcp-project.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-gcp-project/commits)


Terraform module that provisions a MongoDB Atlas Project on Google Cloud Platform (GCP), with optional GCP Cloud KMS
encryption-at-rest integration. The module follows the CloudOpsWorks hub-spoke architecture, auto-generates
resource names from organizational naming conventions, and delegates project-level configuration (alerts, backup
compliance, maintenance windows, IP access lists) to the underlying
`cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project` sub-module.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

## Overview

This module creates and manages a **MongoDB Atlas Project** using GCP as the cloud provider.
It integrates with the CloudOpsWorks organizational hierarchy (org unit, environment type, environment name,
and spoke) to produce consistent, deterministic resource names across all deployments.

### Key capabilities

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| **Project provisioning** | Creates and configures a MongoDB Atlas project within a target Atlas organization |
| **GCP KMS encryption at rest** | Optionally creates a GCP KMS key ring and crypto key, and wires them to the Atlas encryption-at-rest provider |
| **Naming conventions** | Auto-derives project names from org/env/spoke/region — no manual naming required |
| **Hub-spoke support** | Designed for multi-environment deployments; `is_hub` and `spoke_def` control the topology |
| **Import blocks** | Optional OpenTofu import block generation for brown-field adoption of existing Atlas projects |
| **Alert & backup compliance** | Delegates alert policies, backup compliance, maintenance windows, and IP access lists to the sub-module |

### Architecture

```
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  terraform-module-mongoatlas-gcp-project (this module)        │
│                                                               │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  google_kms_key_ring       (conditional)               │   │
│  │  google_kms_crypto_key     (conditional)               │   │
│  │  google_kms_crypto_key_iam_member (conditional)        │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                        │                                      │
│                        ▼                                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  module "project"                                      │   │
│  │  (cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project)   │   │
│  │                                                        │   │
│  │  • mongodbatlas_project                                │   │
│  │  • mongodbatlas_cloud_provider_access_setup (GCP)      │   │
│  │  • mongodbatlas_encryption_at_rest                     │   │
│  │  • mongodbatlas_alert_configuration                    │   │
│  │  • mongodbatlas_backup_compliance_policy               │   │
│  │  • mongodbatlas_maintenance_window                     │   │
│  │  • mongodbatlas_project_ip_access_list                 │   │
│  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Naming convention

Resource names are derived from the organizational context:

| Token | Source |
|---|---|
| `org_unit` | `var.org.organization_unit` |
| `env_name` | `var.org.environment_name` |
| `env_type` | `var.org.environment_type` |
| `spoke` | `var.spoke_def` |
| `region` | GCP provider region (abbreviated) |

The resulting `system_name_plain` is `{org_unit}-{env_name}-{env_type}-{region}` and is used as the default
Atlas project name unless `var.name` or `var.name_prefix` override it.

### GCP KMS encryption at rest

When `settings.encryption_at_rest.enabled = true`:

1. A GCP KMS **key ring** is created (name: `mongodbatlas-{system_name}-keyring`).
2. A **crypto key** is created inside the ring with `ENCRYPT_DECRYPT` purpose and configurable rotation period.
3. The Atlas-generated GCP **service account** (from cloud provider access setup) is granted the
   `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter` IAM role on the key.
4. The current key version resource ID is passed to the Atlas encryption-at-rest provider config.

> **Important**: The crypto key has `prevent_destroy = true` to protect against accidental key deletion,
> which would permanently lock encrypted MongoDB data.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-gcp-project/releases).


## Terragrunt usage

The following example shows a typical `terragrunt.hcl` deployment using the `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml`
configuration file loaded by the Terragrunt hierarchy.

```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl (generated by boilerplate)
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-gcp-project.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

locals {
  local_vars = yamldecode(file("${get_terragrunt_dir()}/inputs.yaml"))
}

inputs = {
  # -- Supplied by the Terragrunt hierarchy (do NOT set in inputs.yaml) --
  is_hub     = local.is_hub
  spoke_def  = local.spoke_def
  org        = local.org
  extra_tags = local.extra_tags

  # -- Module-specific inputs (set in inputs.yaml) --
  name_prefix       = local.local_vars.name_prefix
  name              = local.local_vars.name
  organization_name = local.local_vars.organization_name
  organization_id   = local.local_vars.organization_id
  settings          = local.local_vars.settings
  generate_import   = local.local_vars.generate_import
}
```

### Minimal deployment (no encryption)

`inputs.yaml`:
```yaml
organization_name: "my-atlas-org"

settings:
  default_alerts_settings: true
  collect_database_specifics_statistics_enabled: true
  data_explorer_enabled: true
  performance_advisor_enabled: true
  schema_advisor_enabled: true
  encryption_at_rest:
    enabled: false
```

### Deployment with GCP KMS encryption at rest

`inputs.yaml`:
```yaml
organization_name: "my-atlas-org"

settings:
  default_alerts_settings: true
  collect_database_specifics_statistics_enabled: true
  data_explorer_enabled: true
  performance_advisor_enabled: true
  schema_advisor_enabled: true
  encryption_at_rest:
    enabled: true
    kms_location: "us-central1"      # defaults to GCP provider region if omitted
    rotation_period: "7776000s"      # 90 days
```

### Deployment with explicit project name and import block generation

`inputs.yaml`:
```yaml
name: "my-custom-atlas-project"
organization_id: "64b1a2c3d4e5f60012345678"

generate_import: true

settings:
  default_alerts_settings: true
  encryption_at_rest:
    enabled: false
```

## Quick Start

## Quick start

1. **Prerequisites**
   - Terraform >= 1.7 or OpenTofu >= 1.7
   - GCP project with Cloud KMS API enabled (if using encryption at rest)
   - MongoDB Atlas API keys with Organization Owner role
   - Terragrunt installed

2. **Initialize the module**
   ```sh
   make init/gcp
   ```

3. **Create your `inputs.yaml`** from the `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml` template:
   ```sh
   cp .boilerplate/inputs.yaml ./my-deployment/inputs.yaml
   ```
   Edit `my-deployment/inputs.yaml` with your organization name and desired settings.

4. **Format and validate**
   ```sh
   make fmt
   make lint
   ```

5. **Deploy**
   ```sh
   cd my-deployment
   terragrunt init
   terragrunt plan
   terragrunt apply
   ```

6. **After the first apply** — if encryption at rest is enabled, note the output
   `project_kms_atlas_service_account`. This is the GCP service account that Atlas uses to
   access the KMS key. It should already have the required IAM binding applied by this module.


## Examples

## Full settings example

The following `inputs.yaml` demonstrates all available `settings` keys:

```yaml
name_prefix: "acme"
organization_name: "acme-atlas-org"

generate_import: false

settings:
  # Project feature flags
  default_alerts_settings: true                           # (Optional) Enable default Atlas alert policies. Default: true.
  collect_database_specifics_statistics_enabled: true     # (Optional) Collect DB-specific stats. Default: true.
  data_explorer_enabled: true                             # (Optional) Enable Data Explorer UI. Default: true.
  extended_storage_sizes_enabled: false                   # (Optional) Allow extended storage sizes. Default: false.
  performance_advisor_enabled: true                       # (Optional) Enable Performance Advisor. Default: true.
  schema_advisor_enabled: true                            # (Optional) Enable Schema Advisor. Default: true.

  # Backup compliance policy (see Atlas provider docs for full schema)
  backup_compliance:
    authorized_email: "dba@acme.com"
    authorized_user_first_name: "John"
    authorized_user_last_name: "Doe"
    copy_protection_enabled: true
    encryption_at_rest_enabled: true
    pit_enabled: true
    restore_window_days: 7

  # Maintenance window
  maintenance:
    day_of_week: 7    # Sunday (1=Monday … 7=Sunday)
    hour_of_day: 3    # 03:00 UTC

  # IP access list entries
  access_list:
    - cidr_block: "10.0.0.0/8"
      comment: "Internal VPC range"

  # Custom alert configurations (Atlas alert shape)
  alerts: []

  # GCP KMS encryption at rest
  encryption_at_rest:
    enabled: true
    key_ring_name: ""         # (Optional) Defaults to "mongodbatlas-{system_name}-keyring"
    crypto_key_name: ""       # (Optional) Defaults to "mongodbatlas-{system_name}-key"
    kms_location: ""          # (Optional) Defaults to GCP provider region
    rotation_period: "7776000s"  # (Optional) Default: "7776000s" (90 days). Format: "{seconds}s"
```



## Makefile Targets
```
Available targets:

  help                                Help screen
  help/all                            Display help for all targets
  help/short                          This help short screen
  init/%                              Initialize the project for a specific cloud provider: %S
  lint                                Lint terraform/opentofu code
  tag                                 Tag the current version

```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_generate_import"></a> [generate\_import](#input\_generate\_import) | (Optional) Generate OpenTofu import blocks for existing Atlas resources | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_name"></a> [name](#input\_name) | (Optional) Explicit name for the MongoDB Atlas project; overrides name\_prefix | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_name_prefix"></a> [name\_prefix](#input\_name\_prefix) | (Optional) Prefix for the name of the resources | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_organization_id"></a> [organization\_id](#input\_organization\_id) | (Optional) The ID of the MongoDB Atlas organization where the project will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_organization_name"></a> [organization\_name](#input\_organization\_name) | (Optional) The name of the MongoDB Atlas organization where the project will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_settings"></a> [settings](#input\_settings) | (Optional) Module settings object controlling project features: backup compliance, maintenance window, IP access list, alert configurations, and GCP KMS encryption at rest | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |
